mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.95% on 2018-09-01 reflects post-tightening plateau
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off behavior, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% on 2018-09-01 reflects tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of UNRATE change signal < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate the signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 509041M on 2018-09-01, HOUST at 1282K on 2018-08-01
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production over a 2-month window signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 108.2317 on 2018-08-01 reflects manufacturing strength
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of INDPRO decline signal < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 149500K on 2018-09-01 reflects strong employment
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or the signal shows zero correlation with forward returns over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates by more than 0.2% month-over-month, the joint signal indicates monetary tightening pressure that constrains equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.95% and CPIAUCSL at 251.994 on 2018-09-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter at an annualized rate above 2.5% signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18511.576B on 2018-04-01 reflects expansion phase
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in < 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in retail sales and housing starts, both rising > 2% month-over-month in the same month, signals strengthening consumer demand and housing activity that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise > 2% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 509041M and HOUST at 1282K on 2018-08-01 reflect demand strength
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with robust consumer activity
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.
